rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Trial; Memorandum And Declaration In Support
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Petitioner Mary Bong's Motion to Set Trial ("Motion"). Petitioner seeks an order specially setting trial in this matter before the end of 2025, in order to comply with the five-year limitation period under Code of Civil Procedure sec. 583.310 ("An action shall be brought to trial within five years after the action is commenced against the defendant"). On May 2, 2025, Petitioner filed Corrected Third Amended Petition For Orders Directing Conveyance Or Transfer Of Property To Probate Estate; To Set Aside Voidable Transactions; To Suspend Administrator; To Suspend Officers And Directors Of JBE; And For An Accounting ("Petition"). On June 3,2025, Petitioner filed Notice of Hearing. On June 26, 2025, Respondent Annie Yi Tang, in her capacity as an individual, filed Objections to the Petition. On June 7, 2025, Respondents, in their capacities as Trustees, filed Opposition/Objection to Petition, and filed a separate Opposition/Objection to Petition in their individual capacities. The Motion to Set Trial is GRANTED. The Motion asks the court to "set the TAP (and any other related matters deemed appropriate) for trial in November or December 2025." (Motion at 4:1-2.) Given the inextricably interrelated issues pending in this matter and in the related case numbers, PTR-19-303148 (James Won Ling Bong Revocable Trust) and PTR-23-306828 (In the Matter of the Fortune 8 Trust), the court will separately issue orders setting trial on all three matters for December 15, 2025, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 206 of the above-entitled court located at 400 McAllister Street, San Francisco, California. On the current record, and within its discretion, the court finds Petitioner has prosecuted the matter before this court with reasonable diligence and that justice will be served by granting this request for special setting. The court acknowledges granting the request to set a trial date before the end of 2025 will reduce the amount of time available to all parties to prepare for trial on the recently-amended Petition and related matters. However, there is no showing on the record that Respondents will be prejudiced by this any more so than Petitioner. (See, Code of Civil Proc. secs. 36(e), 583.130; Salas v. Sears, Roebuck & Co. (1986) 42 Cal. 3d 342, 349; Warner Bros. Ent. Inc. v. Superior Ct. (2018) 29 Cal. App. 5th 243, 266; Dick v. Superior Ct. (1986) 185 Cal. App. 3d 1159, 1163; Dick v. Superior Ct. (1986) 185 Cal. App. 3d 1159.) Movant shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above.

rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
